title: A stepwise intelligence generative method for structured maintenance guidance documents based on knowledge graph augmented LLM

agents.description: A monolithic LLM-based agent that performs multiple tasks: assists in knowledge graph construction (tokenization and entity alignment), generates structured maintenance guidance documents step-by-step using chain-of-thought prompts, and retrieves relevant knowledge from the knowledge graph to enhance generation.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: The system follows a sequential orchestration graph: first constructing the knowledge graph with LLM-assisted tokenization and entity alignment, then building chain-of-thought prompts, and finally performing stepwise retrieval-augmented generation. Each step of the maintenance guidance document is generated in a loop, with the LLM queried iteratively per document section (basic info, resource input, process steps) until all steps are complete.

human_integration.human_integration_description: Users input the maintenance task name to be generated and initiate the generation process via a structured information system interface. The system generates a structured MGD which users can review and modify through a modular layout interface. Human engineers also provide the initial maintenance guidance document database used for knowledge graph construction.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Automatic generation of structured maintenance guidance documents (MGDs) for industrial equipment, specifically Baosteel continuous casting equipment, to support maintenance workers with standardized, professional, and structured operational instructions.
retrieval.problem_description: Preparing maintenance guidance documents (MGDs) for industrial equipment is labor-intensive, requiring experienced engineers to manually write thousands of documents per year. Manual preparation leads to quality inconsistencies and difficulties in structured storage. Existing LLM-based document generation methods suffer from inaccurate content and structures that do not match professional requirements, especially for long, structured, domain-specific documents.
retrieval.proposed_solution: A stepwise intelligent generation method combining knowledge graph retrieval augmented generation (GraphRAG) and chain-of-thought (CoT) prompts. The approach: (1) constructs a professional maintenance knowledge graph using LLM-assisted tokenization and entity alignment; (2) builds CoT prompts aligned with the stepwise structure of MGDs; (3) performs hierarchical graph retrieval combined with edit distance similarity matching to find relevant knowledge; and (4) generates structured MGDs step-by-step by combining retrieved graph entity content with CoT prompts as enhanced inputs to the LLM (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct).
retrieval.research_methodology_description: The method is applied and evaluated in a real-world case study using 25,136 Baosteel continuous casting equipment maintenance guidance documents. 98 randomly selected documents form the test set. The proposed graph-enhanced stepwise method is compared against a traditional single-prompt method and a graph-only retrieval method using content accuracy (Rouge, BLEU-1), structural accuracy, and generation efficiency metrics.

decision_quality_improvement: Graph-enhanced method achieves average Rouge-1 F1 of 0.750 vs. 0.746 for single-prompt and 0.646 for graph-only method.
Graph-enhanced method achieves precision of 0.740 vs. 0.675 for single-prompt and 0.774 for graph-only method.
Graph-enhanced method achieves BLEU-1 of 0.765 vs. 0.772 for single-prompt and 0.658 for graph-only method.
Structural accuracy of 100% for graph-enhanced method vs. 65.31% for single-prompt method and 100% for graph-only method.
Graph-enhanced method reduces outliers and improves stability compared to single-prompt and graph-only methods.
response_time_impact: Graph-enhanced stepwise method averages 217.42s per document generation vs. 269.63s for single-prompt method, a ~20% improvement in generation efficiency.
Graph-only method is far faster (3.69s average) as it does not call the LLM, but at the cost of lower recall and F1.
reliability_metrics: Structural accuracy: graph-enhanced method 100%, single-prompt method 65.31% (34 out of 98 documents had structural deviations).
Graph-enhanced method shows fewer low-value outliers in accuracy and F1 score box plots compared to single-prompt and graph-only methods.

ce_technique_effectiveness.retrieval-augmented-generation: GraphRAG significantly improves content precision (0.740 vs 0.675) and structural accuracy (100% vs 65.31%) compared to single-prompt method; reduces dependence on graph coverage compared to graph-only method.
ce_technique_effectiveness.chain-of-thought: CoT prompts enable stepwise structured generation, achieving 100% structural accuracy and improving generation efficiency by ~20% over single-prompt method by decomposing long document generation into manageable steps.
ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning: Providing matched similar documents as sample prompts in single-prompt method achieves high recall (0.876) but lower precision and structural accuracy compared to graph-enhanced method.

failure_modes: When graph matching knowledge is dissimilar to the task to be generated, generated results are highly dependent on input prompt and may deviate significantly.
Graph-only method shows large instability (lower recall, more low-value outliers) when no highly similar knowledge exists in the graph.
Single-prompt method tends to directly copy content from prompt samples rather than reasoning, and frequently fails to produce correctly structured output (34.69% structural failure rate).
limitations: Generation accuracy largely depends on the quality and completeness of the constructed knowledge graph.
When there is a large gap between matched graph knowledge and the task to be generated, the model lacks professional knowledge to compensate.

Evaluation metrics (precision, recall, F1, BLEU-1) are relatively simple for professional document generation tasks; a more comprehensive evaluation system covering fluency, credibility, and completeness is needed.
Lack of a professional vocabulary tokenization tool with good effects necessitates use of unit-word-based Rouge-1 and BLEU-1 indices.

baseline_methods: Traditional single-prompt method (model definition + task description + edit-distance-retrieved document examples)
Graph-only retrieval method (hierarchical retrieval + chain-of-thought retrieval without LLM generation)
identified_research_gaps: Improving coverage and accuracy of the knowledge graph to reduce dependence on graph quality and completeness for generation accuracy
Introducing more professional knowledge retrieval and prompts in the generation process to improve stability when matched knowledge is dissimilar to the target task
Developing a more comprehensive evaluation system for generated documents covering fluency, credibility, and completeness beyond simple reference-text comparison metrics
Building a complete evaluation system that can be applied to automatic review of model-generated results or fine-tuning training of professional models
